name: AG OS Coordinator Runtime
goal: Provide Gurnoor with a private always-on AG OS command center while preserving exact approval gates for every external or production side effect.
knownFacts: PR #128 merged the authenticated owner command console and dependency-free coordinator into main at merge commit 81866ec6b4e127db254ba0ba15bfc9ee7cf7f9dd.
The intended owner-only runtime is a localhost-bound Node.js service behind an authenticated TLS reverse proxy on the existing Hetzner VPS.

Read-only preflight on 2026-07-12 verified host agdigitalz-prod at 5.78.87.188, Docker 29.1.3, Docker Compose 2.40.3, 47 GiB available disk, 2.3 GiB available memory, free loopback port 8787, and no existing /opt/ag-os path.

The host Node.js version is 18.19.1 and the production Caddy proxy runs inside infra-proxy-1; the first activation therefore uses an isolated pinned Node 20 container and an SSH tunnel without modifying host Node, Caddy, DNS, or the existing infra Compose stack.
No VPS deployment, hostname, DNS change, owner token, dashboard password, live monitor, or production connector is active from this record.
scope: Prepare the coordinator for private Hetzner operation.
Provide backup, restore, monitoring, post-deploy verification, incident, and credential-rotation procedures.
Track coordinator-specific production readiness separately from Social Media Management System readiness.
outOfScope: Do not deploy without exact approval naming the target and source commit.
Do not store, rotate, revoke, or expose credentials from source control or chat.
Do not change DNS, domains, firewall, account settings, billing, or production data.
Do not authorize posting, messaging, paid actions, customer data, social OAuth, or n8n activation.

risks.risk: An internet-exposed operator console could disclose internal operating evidence or accept unauthorized commands.
risks.mitigation: Bind the coordinator to localhost, require a bearer token, require a separate reverse-proxy password, use TLS, and fail closed when either authentication layer is absent.

risks.risk: File-backed runtime state could be lost or corrupted during deployment or rollback.
risks.mitigation: Create an integrity-hashed state snapshot before change, retain the previous source commit, and verify restored state before service restart.

risks.risk: A healthy process could still be operationally unsafe.
risks.mitigation: Post-deploy validation checks unauthenticated rejection, authenticated status, production blocker state, and exact source version before acceptance.

qualityGates: Foundation validation, dashboard check, boot check, secret scan, tests, and diff check pass for the exact candidate.
State backup and isolated restore drill pass.
Service refuses to start without owner authentication.
Unauthenticated API calls receive 401 and authenticated status succeeds.
Monitoring and rollback commands are documented before deployment.
Exact deployment approval is active before the first VPS mutation.
securityReview: Owner-only internal runtime. Secrets remain external to Git and chat. The first activation binds the container to host loopback only and requires an authenticated SSH tunnel plus the bearer token. Caddy, DNS, and public exposure remain out of scope, and all external actions remain separately approval-controlled.
costTracking: No new paid service or billing change is authorized. Reuse of existing Hetzner capacity requires confirmation that it adds no new charge; any added service or cost requires separate approval.
deploymentPlan: First activation target: exact reviewed main commit on agdigitalz-prod (5.78.87.188), built as the pinned Node 20 Docker image from ops/docker-compose.hetzner.yml, with state under /var/lib/ag-os/state and port 8787 bound only to 127.0.0.1. Access is through an owner SSH tunnel. Do not modify host Node, the infra Compose stack, Caddy, firewall, or DNS. Snapshot state first and roll back the isolated container, source, and state if acceptance fails.
